rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-working-directory boundaries always open new; evidence travels by disk.
Source-verification maintainer block on any student-facing file with practitioner citations.

platform_claims_checked.claim: gitignored files (`CLAUDE.local.md`, `.claude/memory/`) don't ride into a worktree because git doesn't see them
platform_claims_checked.note: Matches check_platform_and_boundaries.md §7b verbatim: 'gitignored files (CLAUDE.local.md, .claude/memory/) don't cross worktrees by git — copy at fork time and accept independent divergence after.'
Matches check_platform_and_boundaries.md §6d: '~/.claude/CLAUDE.md (user-level, cross-repo)' is one of the three auto-load filenames. Claim is accurate.

platform_claims_checked.claim: AE101's default chose `cp` at fork time — your M1/M3 evidence rode forward
platform_claims_checked.note: Internal curriculum architecture claim about AE101 design, not a platform capability claim. Consistent with §7b's documented cp-at-fork pattern.

citations_checked.claim: All practitioner postures are guesses — none of them have written this up
citations_checked.note: File explicitly disclaims citation status for all four practitioner readings. No source URL or type label required — these are presented as guesses, not evidence-ladder claims. check_research_claims.md §11 fires on 'named practitioner + specific URL + load-bearing number'; none of these conditions hold here.

open_todos.note: Maintainer-block open item: 'Live-test whether ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md user-level auto-load is the actual current Cherny default (his own rules-file location).' The auto-load behavior of ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md itself is verified correct (§6d). The open item is about what Boris Cherny personally uses — a stylistic/attribution refinement, not a platform-claim correctness gap. Non-blocking TODO.
